commit.message:
fix: write barrier/bulk acks directly under writeMu, not via barrierCh

The barrierCh priority channel doesn't help when sendLoop is blocked
on writeMu (held by BulkSync per-session writes). The ack sits in
barrierCh while sendLoop can't read it. Write acks directly under
writeMu instead — they'll be sent as soon as the current BulkSync
per-session write releases the lock (<1ms).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: read PORT from env for railway deployment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(runtime): auto-install missing npm packages for SSR/API routes (#2093) (#2238)

* feat(runtime): auto-install missing npm packages for SSR/API routes (#2093)

Extract auto-install coordination into shared AutoInstaller struct and
thread it into PersistentIsolate. When SSR or API route modules import a
missing package, the isolate now detects "Cannot find module" errors,
installs the package via pm::add, creates a fresh V8 runtime, and retries
(up to 5 times). This mirrors the existing browser-side auto-install
behavior for /@deps/ requests.

- New auto_installer.rs: dedup, blacklist, global lock, broadcaster
- Refactored DevServerState to use Option<Arc<AutoInstaller>>
- Removed standalone auto_install_package() (~160 lines)
- Added retry loop in isolate_event_loop with fresh runtime per retry
- parse_missing_package() helper with 6 unit tests
- Shared Arc<AutoInstaller> coordinates both browser and V8 paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(runtime): address review findings for auto-install (#2093)

- Extract shared MISSING_MODULE_PREFIX/SUFFIX constants between
  module_loader.rs and parse_missing_package() to prevent format drift
- Extract try_auto_install() helper to DRY the 3 identical retry blocks
- Add log when already-installed package is skipped (debugging aid)
- Add tests: shared constant integration, try_auto_install edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(runtime): correct SSR fallback detection placement after rebase (#2093)

The SSR fallback detection logic (framework vs plain JS app) was
placed in the extract_api_handler error handler during conflict
resolution. Moved it to the correct location: the SSR init module
loading error handler where @vertz/ui-server/ssr fails to load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

fix(config): preserve computed metric name keys in YAML normalization

of truth.

v76: Sphere grab threshold = radius (not radius*2)

Only grab handle sphere when pinch is inside the sphere volume.
Prevents drift from repeated pinches slightly outside the sphere.
Browser version uses ray-sphere intersection (same precision).

commit.message:
Simplify README — CLAUDE_PROMPT is the entry point

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: consolidate entry point discovery, remove compiler.entryFile (#2230) (#2235)

* docs: document src/server.ts auto-discovery convention (#2230)

Add a Project Structure guide explaining how vertz dev auto-detects
entry points and determines app type (ui-only, api-only, full-stack).
Clarify the distinction between compiler.entryFile (codegen) and
src/server.ts (runtime API server entry). Document the deliberate
decision to not add a server.entryFile config option (convention over
configuration).

- New page: project-structure.mdx in Getting Started group
- Add Warning to codegen.mdx below compiler.entryFile config block
- Replace competing project structure section in installation.mdx with link

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: format project-structure.mdx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(compiler): remove compiler.entryFile config option (#2230)

The compiler scans all files in sourceDir regardless of entryFile —
it was never used for actual file analysis. Replace the config-based
entry detection in incremental compilation with convention-based
detection (server.ts, server.tsx, app.ts, app.tsx in src/).

This eliminates the confusing distinction between compiler.entryFile
(codegen) and src/server.ts (runtime), consolidating both into a
single file-convention-based discovery mechanism.

- Remove entryFile from CompilerConfig interface and resolveConfig
- Replace CategorizeOptions.entryFile with ENTRY_FILE_NAMES set
- Remove entryFile from CLI defaults, orchestrators, examples, templates
- Update all tests and documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings — remove stale entryFile refs, add .tsx tests (#2230)

- Remove entryFile from CLI README config example and reference table
- Remove entryFile from packages/site codegen.mdx config example
- Add clarifying comment for /api/ guard in incremental categorizer
- Add test coverage for server.tsx and app.tsx entry file variants
- Add adversarial review for phase 2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: 修复支付、推荐、秒杀、MCP助手模块的多个 bug

支付模块：
- 金额 yuan→fen 转换补充 math.Round 防精度丢失
- 流水号随机位数从 3 位提升至 6 位
- paymentnotifylogic/mockpaylogic：过期/失败分支操作移入事务保证原子性
- getpaymentstatuslogic：补充归属校验防越权查询
- refundlogic：退款状态改为根据渠道 Sync 标志动态设置
- channel：RefundResponse 新增 Sync 字段；Mock 渠道设置 Sync=true

推荐模块（猜你喜欢）：
- 缓存键补充 pageSize 维度，避免跨 page size 缓存碰撞
- rerank：重写两阶段分散逻辑，修复多轮状态共享 bug

推荐模块（凑满减）：
- 缓存键改用 int64(math.Round(cartTotal*100)) 避免浮点截断
- strategyAssociated：N+1 查询改为 FindByMainProductIds 批量查询
- deduplicateAndRank：删除未使用的 threshold 参数
- strategyPriceGap：gap==0 时增加除零保护
- model：新增 CombinationProductModel.FindByMainProductIds 方法

行为上报：
- reportbehaviorlogic：补充 ProductID/CategoryID <= 0 校验

秒杀模块（新增）：
- Redis Lua 原子扣库存 + 限购校验
- Kafka 异步下单 + Consumer 事务：orders + product库存 + seckill_activity库存
- 修复：Consumer 事务中补充 seckillActivityModel.DecrStock
- 修复：ListSeckillActivities 改为读 Redis 实时库存

MCP 智能助手：
- execGetCombinationDiscounts：N+1 改为 FindByIds 批量查询
- streamDoubao：toolCalls 改用 map 索引，消除潜在越界 panic
- doubao.go：使用带 60s 超时的 doubaoHTTPClient 替代 http.DefaultClient
- chatstreamlogic：无工具调用时直接写 SSE，消除双倍 API 调用

* refactor: extract shared normalize/check from normalize_contents

Rename `normalize` component to `normalize_contents` and extract a
shared `normalize(src_dir, out_dir, validator)` core function and
`check(src_dir, validator)` public function, preparing for reuse
by the upcoming `normalize_queries` component.

* feat: add query schema validation via normalize_queries stage

Add a built-in QuerySchema (query-schema.yaml) that validates
user query files, and a normalize_queries pipeline stage that
gates compose with schema validation.

- Add query-schema.yaml defining from/grouped/select structure
- Add normalize_queries component and build_query_validator
- Insert normalize_queries stage between normalize_contents and compose
- Compose now reads from normalized_queries_dir
- Skip __-prefixed keys in compose to ignore __build_report

* refactor: derive component name from fn.__name__, remove on_stage()

Component now captures fn.__name__ as its name automatically,
eliminating the redundant on_stage() call in stage factories.
Rename StageResult to ComponentResult since the result describes
component execution, not stage orchestration.

commit.message:
Increase file size limit to 500MB and add extraction progress

Full textbooks (OpenStax, etc.) are commonly 200-500MB. Raised the
extraction and upload limits from 20MB/100MB to 500MB. PDF extraction
now reports page-by-page progress via a callback, and the chapter
detection spinner shows "Reading pages… 150 / 4,411" so users know
it's working on large files. Yields to the UI thread every 25 pages
to keep the progress display responsive.

* Fix filter format compatibility when switching LinkedIn ↔ Base Konekt

6 critical bugs fixed: filters with LinkedIn numeric IDs were silently
ignored by Apollo when switching to Base Konekt mode.

Fixes in buildSearchParams:
- Company: send names array for database (was sending numeric IDs as text)
- Industry: send names array for database (was sending IDs as keyword tags)
- School: send {id, name} objects for database (Apollo rejects pure numeric IDs)
- Skills: always send name/keywords for database (Apollo regex filters out numbers)
- Job Title: always include name field (Apollo needs text, not LinkedIn IDs)
- Company Location: send {id, name} for database (same issue)

Before: User fills 'Google' in LinkedIn (ID: 162479), switches to Base Konekt
  → Apollo searches for '162479' as text → 0 results
After: Apollo receives 'Google' as text → correct results

Previous values (0.02, 0.05) were in sim space but too small.
VR_SCALE=0.05 means: sim 0.05 = VR 2.5mm (unnoticeable).

fix(runtime): skip CSS transform for test files in vertz test (#2237)

* fix(runtime): skip CSS transform for test files in vertz test (#2141)

The native compiler's CSS transform replaces static css() calls with
plain class name maps at compile time. This breaks test files that
assert on the runtime css() behavior (e.g., checking the .css property
or document.adoptedStyleSheets).

Add skip_css_transform option to CompileOptions and detect test files
(*.test.ts, *.spec.ts, __tests__/) in VertzPlugin to preserve css()
calls for the runtime implementation.

- Remove duplicate in plugin::vertz, import shared version
- Add *.e2e.{ts,tsx} and *.local.{ts,tsx} patterns
- Add comprehensive tests for all patterns in both modules

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix chapter detection for academic textbooks and prevent AI cutoffs

Three changes:
1. PDF extraction now preserves line structure by tracking Y-coordinates
   of text items, so chapter headings appear on their own lines instead
   of being concatenated with page numbers and author names.
2. Chapter detection now tries TOC-based parsing first — finds the Table
   of Contents, extracts numbered entries, then locates them in the body.
   Falls back to line-based heuristic patterns. Also adds flexible bare-
   number and period-less Roman numeral patterns for academic textbooks.
3. Adds a 200K character guard on single-note generation to prevent the
   AI from hitting token limits and cutting off mid-sentence. Shows a
   toast guiding the user to chapter mode for full coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add graceful shutdown for debug threads to prevent process hang (#994) (#995)

* fix: add graceful shutdown for debug threads to prevent process hang (#994)

The deadlock-detector and signal-handler threads spawned by
init_debug_handlers() had no shutdown mechanism — they ran forever with
discarded JoinHandles. After discovery work finished, these threads
(and signal_hook's registered handlers) could prevent the host process
from exiting cleanly.

Add shutdown_debug_handlers() that sets an atomic shutdown flag, closes
the signal_hook Handle to unblock the signal iterator, and joins both
threads with a bounded timeout. Expose cleanup_discovery_lib() as an
FFI function for Deno/Node callers.

Enclosure v5: corrected Jetson hole positions, standoff fix, print lessons

- Jetson mounting holes corrected to STEP-verified coords: (4,19),(4,77),(90,19),(90,77)
  spacing 86x58mm — previous x=96/y=4 values caused misaligned standoffs
- Standoff base pillar now protected from mating recess subtraction so they
  can't disconnect from the plate (root cause of floating standoff print failure)
- Added M3 nut trap pockets (d=6mm, 3mm deep) under each join boss; BOM updated
  to M3x14 — M3x12 only left 1mm for nut engagement
- Top shell: 2 volumes (was 4) — replaced hull() gussets with box ribs,
  ceiling bosses integrated into outer shell diff
- End gcode: relative Z50 lift before XY park (absolute Z250 could clip soft endstop)
- CLAUDE.md: added print monitoring rules, end gcode rules, OpenSCAD design rules,
  dimension verification workflow
- design_assumptions.md: tracks all verified vs assumed dimensions and known issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
